text: Merdeka Mengajar as a Teacher Professional Development Program through Technology-Based Resource Use in Vocational Schools. SMK Negeri 1 Dlanggu Mojokerto Abstract In Indonesia, teachers in vocational high schools (SMK) still use conventional instructional materials and approaches inherited from the past, which does not relate to current industry requirements. This study aimed to investigate the influence of Merdeka Mengajar programs to the use of technology-based resource through a professional learning community that subsequently affects teacher’s instructional approaches in SMK Negeri 1 Dlanggu Mojokerto, Mojokerto regency, East Java. This study used qualitative case study research design. Data was collected through interviews, observations and documentation analysis to describe teachers’ experiences and perceptions toward the program, use of technology-based resource and effects toward their instructional approaches. The results show that teachers in SMK can access a wide range of e-resource through a digital platform that can help them to improve their teaching instructional approaches to match current requirement from industry. They also stated that the program enabled them to join professional learning community. Nevertheless, they stated several constraints including infrastructure, digital literacy, and curriculum design that had not been synchronized with the e-resource on the platform. In general, teachers still had a positive response regarding the program that enabled them to increase students engagement and improve their instructional approaches. This study concluded that despite its effectiveness in improving teacher capacity, the Merdeka Mengajar program still required technical supports in the form of hardware and software repair, curriculum design that was responsive to the e-resource on the platform and infrastructure improvement.
